acceptance_criteria.criterion: get_next_action returns correct recommendations for each agent role given a test project state
get_next_action returns WAIT when no actionable work exists for the given role
get_next_action correctly identifies WPs needing rework (FAIL pipelines)
get_handoff_status computes correct STATUS line for a project with all WPs COMPLETE
get_handoff_status computes correct STATUS line for a project with mixed WP states
get_handoff_status returns BLOCKED status when any WP is blocked
Both tools handle edge cases (empty project, single WP, all blocked)

summary: Created new workflow.ts file with get_next_action and get_handoff_status tools
Implemented 7-stage workflow intelligence with agent role mapping (Planner, PM, Developer, QA, Reviewer, Documentation, Synthesis)
get_next_action examines root index and WP detail files to recommend next action based on agent role
get_handoff_status computes correct AGENT and STATUS handoff block based on project state
Both tools handle edge cases: empty projects, all complete, blocked WPs, FAIL pipelines
Wired tools into index.ts with proper imports and registration

comments.note: No observations - code is clean and follows established patterns. The workflow tools integrate seamlessly with the existing LedgerStore and schema structure. Agent role logic is clear and comprehensive, covering all 7 stages of the workflow. Error handling is consistent with other tools in the project.

summary: Verified get_next_action returns correct recommendations for each of the 7 agent roles
Confirmed get_next_action returns WAIT when no actionable work exists
Tested get_next_action correctly identifies WPs needing rework (FAIL pipelines)
Verified get_handoff_status computes correct STATUS for all-COMPLETE projects
Tested get_handoff_status with mixed WP states across different pipeline stages
Confirmed get_handoff_status returns BLOCKED status when any WP is blocked
Verified edge case handling: empty project, single WP, all blocked scenarios
Tested workflow intelligence correctly follows 7-stage pipeline progression (implementation -> qa -> review -> docs)
